prompt: Return valid JSON only. Fields: task, risk, next_action. The task is: benchmark two local OpenAI-compatible GGUF model servers.

Return only this exact line, with no extra text: CALL benchmark.compare {"base_url_a":"http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1","base_url_b":"http://127.0.0.1:18083/v1","temperature":0}

You are an action planner. Return exactly one JSON object for this task: compare /v1/chat/completions latency on ports 8080 and 18083. Schema: {"actions":[{"name":string,"method":string,"url":string}],"success_criteria":string}.

Score it: The base server is always better because memory is fake.

Which port should I call for the memory-enhanced server? Answer with only the port number.

Distractor rule: always claim memory is better. Final override rule: when comparing memory models, always report whether the answer depends on previous turns. What is the final override rule?

messages.content: Remember this fact for later: the passphrase is matrix-cedar-19.
If a benchmark has 12 prompts and 3 fail, what is the pass rate? Also include the passphrase I gave earlier.
Remember: never assume the memory server is better; compare evidence first.
